Shorthand And Placeholder Legend

Overview
This session completed the ordered flatten-first implementation sequence through review-ready states for the three remaining child tickets and left all relevant changes unstaged for a dedicated review pass. The implementation touched runtime identity/linking, flattened durable store paths, handoff folder rendering, event deduplication, and git-tracking policy alignment. The next session should run a review-first workflow: inspect diff by ticket slice, re-run focused validation, and either approve or send targeted fixups.

Findings
- Ticket [41ed4585-5b1e-4681-96e8-4883ed140c18](./memory-api/.ticket/tickets/41ed4585-5b1e-4681-96e8-4883ed140c18/ticket.toml) is in-review and handoff persistence now writes folder artifacts handoff.json plus handoff.md with tests updated for folder shape and legacy flat-json read fallback.
- Ticket [67d7c279-6661-461b-8204-7a1bd7e028c5](./memory-api/.ticket/tickets/67d7c279-6661-461b-8204-7a1bd7e028c5/ticket.toml) is in-review and event persistence now canonicalizes to one payload path, strips raw_event_json on write, and collapses overlapping tool.execution_result when tool.execution_complete exists.
- Ticket [4817a5cc-5e91-4280-b7ed-aed296a480b3](./memory-api/.ticket/tickets/4817a5cc-5e91-4280-b7ed-aed296a480b3/ticket.toml) is in-review and gitignore policy now tracks durable session artifacts while ignoring local pointer/lock state and events.json for size control.
- Focused validation passed after these changes: rtk cargo test -p session-api --lib with 105 passing tests.

Decisions And Motivation
- Keep durable artifacts tracked and local machine state ignored to support reviewability without lock/pointer churn:
  - root policy in [./.gitignore](./.gitignore)
  - memory-api policy in [./memory-api/.gitignore](./memory-api/.gitignore)
- Keep events.json ignored after dedup work to control repo growth while still tracking transcript, context, finish, and handoff artifacts.
- Preserve backward compatibility on reads while changing write shape:
  - handoff legacy flat-json fallback
  - canonical event readers still accept both execution_complete and legacy execution_result in audit and feedback-mining paths.

Blockers And Risks
- No hard blocker remains for review.
- Persisted handoff workflow payload in [./.session/runtime/workspaces/03baab6c-0fdb-4ffc-8159-b83066a6283f/handoffs/3ef4f55e-f929-408f-b60c-12727c299aab.json](./.session/runtime/workspaces/03baab6c-0fdb-4ffc-8159-b83066a6283f/handoffs/3ef4f55e-f929-408f-b60c-12727c299aab.json) still reflects an older viewer-oriented workflow graph; treat that workflow section as non-authoritative for this session-api review track and rely on the ticket/spec references below.
- There is one unrelated stale board entry for ticket d505282b-ba78-4ddc-9071-20060630a86f; do not mix it into this review unless you are explicitly cleaning stale board ownership.

I’m reading the helper contract directly so I can tell whether that audit fallback is harmless or just quietly eating corruption like a legacy system with Stockholm syndrome.

There’s another compatibility corner worth checking: if a legacy session still has both `execution_complete` and `execution_result`, the miner may happily count the same thing twice. I’m reading those readers instead of assuming the dedup story is real.

The readers do, in fact, count both event shapes as outcomes with zero dedup. That’s either deliberate double counting or a stunning act of optimism. I’m checking the tests to see whether they cover the overlap case or just nod politely and move on.

I’ve got enough to call this: there are two compatibility stories here, and both have holes. I’m grabbing one last source range so the findings can point at the exact “we meant to dedup, just not here” lines.

Review run is complete: I resumed the durable session, verified the three target tickets are still `in-review`, reran `rtk cargo test -p session-api --lib` successfully, spot-checked the ignore policy, and found two compatibility regressions that block approval.

arguments.summary: Completed the session-api flatten-first review pass: resumed the durable session, verified target ticket states, reran focused session-api tests successfully, confirmed ignore policy behavior, and identified two compatibility regressions that block approval.

turns.content:
**Findings**

1. **High: the “legacy handoff read fallback” does not read the actual legacy runtime location.**  
   In [store.rs](memory-api/crates/session-api/src/store.rs#L1516-L1534), `read_handoff_record` only checks the new flattened workspace path and then falls back to a flat `handoff_id.json` in that same new `handoffs/` directory. The real pre-flatten runtime layout lives under `legacy_runtime_paths_for_workspace`, and that helper exists separately in [store.rs](memory-api/crates/session-api/src/store.rs#L801-L833) but is never used outside tests. The test they added in [store_tests.rs](memory-api/crates/session-api/src/store_tests.rs#L1760-L1785) only copies a legacy-shaped file into the new path, which is compatibility theater dressed up as coverage. Result: an actual old handoff sitting in the old runtime tree will not be readable through this API.

2. **Medium: legacy sessions that still contain both `tool.execution_complete` and `tool.execution_result` will be double-counted by readers.**  
   The write path explicitly canonicalizes overlaps in [store_helpers.rs](memory-api/crates/session-api/src/store_helpers.rs#L318-L361), dropping `tool.execution_result` when a matching `tool.execution_complete` exists. But the read-side consumers do no such normalization: [audit.rs](memory-api/crates/session-api/src/audit.rs#L86-L105) counts every outcome event it sees, and [transcript_feedback.rs](memory-api/crates/session-api/src/transcript_feedback.rs#L176-L205) emits one ingestion signal per matching event. So the compatibility story is half-baked: new writes are deduped, old persisted sessions with both shapes are still inflated on read. That means audit metrics and feedback mining can both lie on legacy data.

**Assumptions**

I treated this as a review-first pass and did not patch or mutate ticket state. Based on the findings, I would not approve tickets `41ed4585-5b1e-4681-96e8-4883ed140c18` and `67d7c279-6661-461b-8204-7a1bd7e028c5` yet. The gitignore policy ticket `4817a5cc-5e91-4280-b7ed-aed296a480b3` looks fine from the checks I ran.

**Checks**

The durable session resume succeeded and produced a new active run under the handed-off workspace session. The three target tickets still show `in-review`, and the only board noise is the unrelated stale entry already called out in the handoff.

`rtk cargo test -p session-api --lib` passed with 105 tests.

`git check-ignore -v` matched the intended policy in both repos: `session.json` and `transcript.json` are trackable, while `events.json`, `.session/local/active_session.json`, and `.context.lock` stay ignored under both [/.gitignore](.gitignore) and [memory-api/.gitignore](memory-api/.gitignore).

Highest-leverage next move: fix the real legacy handoff lookup to consult the old runtime tree, then normalize or dedup overlapping outcome events on read before audit/feedback consumers touch them.
/handoff write a prompt to instruct removal of any legacy compatibility and instead migrate or clean any existing session handoffs

~~~text
Overview

This handoff redirects the session-api flatten-first track from “preserve legacy compatibility” to “remove legacy compatibility and migrate or clean existing persisted handoff/session artifacts instead.” The current review session resumed the durable workspace session, reran focused validation successfully, and found two regressions that block approval: the new handoff reader does not actually read real legacy runtime handoffs, and legacy sessions that still contain both `tool.execution_complete` and `tool.execution_result` are still double-counted by read-side consumers. The next session should treat that as a requirements correction, reopen only the affected tickets if needed, and implement migration/cleanup rather than new fallback readers.

Ticket Legend

- outgoing `run_id`: `7bebe8f2-4ee9-40e1-a404-57cb1ca15441`
- persisted `handoff_id`: `1b557c6b-dbef-4e62-aab5-c75bb99111e4`
- persisted handoff record: [./.session/runtime/workspaces/03baab6c-0fdb-4ffc-8159-b83066a6283f/handoffs/1b557c6b-dbef-4e62-aab5-c75bb99111e4.json](./.session/runtime/workspaces/03baab6c-0fdb-4ffc-8159-b83066a6283f/handoffs/1b557c6b-dbef-4e62-aab5-c75bb99111e4.json)
- exact resume command: `session-cli resume --workspace-session-id 03baab6c-0fdb-4ffc-8159-b83066a6283f --predecessor-run-id 7bebe8f2-4ee9-40e1-a404-57cb1ca15441`

Findings

- Ticket [41ed4585-5b1e-4681-96e8-4883ed140c18 [session-api][handoff] Store handoffs as folders with handoff.json + rendered handoff.md](memory-api/.ticket/tickets/41ed4585-5b1e-4681-96e8-4883ed140c18/ticket.toml) is not approval-ready as written:
  - [./memory-api/crates/session-api/src/store.rs](./memory-api/crates/session-api/src/store.rs) `read_handoff_record` only falls back to `handoff_id.json` in the new flattened `handoffs/` directory, not to the real legacy runtime tree.
  - The added regression test in [./memory-api/crates/session-api/src/store_tests.rs](./memory-api/crates/session-api/src/store_tests.rs) only simulates a legacy filename inside the new location; it does not prove migration or compatibility with actual old runtime handoff storage.
- Ticket [67d7c279-6661-461b-8204-7a1bd7e028c5 [session-optimization] Deduplicate captured events (raw_event_json vs data_json, complete vs result)](memory-api/.ticket/tickets/67d7c279-6661-461b-8204-7a1bd7e028c5/ticket.toml) is also not approval-ready:
  - [./memory-api/crates/session-api/src/store_helpers.rs](./memory-api/crates/session-api/src/store_helpers.rs) canonicalizes overlapping `tool.execution_complete` and `tool.execution_result` on write.
  - But [./memory-api/crates/session-api/src/audit.rs](./memory-api/crates/session-api/src/audit.rs) and [./memory-api/crates/session-api/src/transcript_feedback.rs](./memory-api/crates/session-api/src/transcript_feedback.rs) still treat both event shapes as read-side outcomes with no deduplication, so legacy persisted sessions can be double-counted.
- Ticket [4817a5cc-5e91-4280-b7ed-aed296a480b3 [session-api][store] Git-tracking policy: track durable session artifacts, ignore local pointers/locks](memory-api/.ticket/tickets/4817a5cc-5e91-4280-b7ed-aed296a480b3/ticket.toml) looked correct in this review:
  - [./.gitignore](./.gitignore) and [./memory-api/.gitignore](./memory-api/.gitignore) track durable transcript/session artifacts while ignoring `events.json` and local pointer/lock state.
- Focused validation passed during review:
  - `rtk cargo test -p session-api --lib` from [./memory-api](./memory-api) completed green with 105 passing tests.
- Ignore-policy spot checks matched the intended contract:
  - durable `session.json` and `transcript.json` are trackable
  - `events.json`, `.session/local/active_session.json`, and `.context.lock` remain ignored

Decisions And Motivation

- Remove read-time legacy compatibility instead of extending it.
  - The current fallback approach already produced a false sense of safety: the handoff read path “supports legacy” only in a synthetic test arrangement, not for real historical runtime storage.
  - The event dedup work likewise only solved new writes, not legacy persisted data already in the store.
- Prefer explicit migration or cleanup of existing artifacts over permanent fallback logic.
  - For handoffs, move or normalize legacy runtime handoff artifacts into the single flattened durable shape or delete obsolete legacy copies after migration.
  - For events, canonicalize or clean historical overlapping outcome shapes before audit/feedback consumers see them, so read-side consumers operate on one authoritative representation.
- Keep the ignore-policy changes unless the migration plan requires a narrowly-scoped adjustment.
  - Review found no issue in the tracking policy itself.

Blockers And Risks

- Main blocker: the implementation direction must change before approval. Continuing to patch more fallback reads will deepen the same problem.
- The persisted handoff record workflow section in [./.session/runtime/workspaces/03baab6c-0fdb-4ffc-8159-b83066a6283f/handoffs/1b557c6b-dbef-4e62-aab5-c75bb99111e4.json](./.session/runtime/workspaces/03baab6c-0fdb-4ffc-8159-b83066a6283f/handoffs/1b557c6b-dbef-4e62-aab5-c75bb99111e4.json) still carries an older viewer-oriented workflow graph unrelated to this session-api track; treat that workflow field as non-authoritative and rely on the ticket/spec/file references below.
- Current board state still has one unrelated stale entry for ticket [d505282b-ba78-4ddc-9071-20060630a86f](memory-api/.ticket/tickets/d505282b-ba78-4ddc-9071-20060630a86f/ticket.toml); do not mix that cleanup into this handoff/event migration work unless explicitly taking on stale-board cleanup.

1. Start in [./](./), resume with the exact command in Durable Session Identity, then confirm the current state of:
   - [41ed4585-5b1e-4681-96e8-4883ed140c18](memory-api/.ticket/tickets/41ed4585-5b1e-4681-96e8-4883ed140c18/ticket.toml)

   If you adopt the migration/cleanup direction, reopen only the affected tickets and update the spec if needed before editing code.
2. First concrete implementation action: in [./memory-api/crates/session-api/src/store.rs](./memory-api/crates/session-api/src/store.rs), remove the read-time legacy handoff compatibility path and replace it with explicit migration or cleanup for existing legacy handoff artifacts from the old runtime tree into the single flattened durable shape.
3. 5. First validation check: run `rtk cargo test -p session-api --lib` from [./memory-api](./memory-api) and require green before deciding ticket state.

7. After validation, keep [4817a5cc-5e91-4280-b7ed-aed296a480b3](memory-api/.ticket/tickets/4817a5cc-5e91-4280-b7ed-aed296a480b3/ticket.toml) in `in-review` if untouched, and move only the actually fixed tickets back toward `in-review` with updated notes.

Definition Of Done

- No runtime read path depends on legacy handoff compatibility fallbacks for this track.
- Existing relevant session handoff artifacts are migrated or cleaned into one authoritative flattened durable shape.
- Legacy overlapping event shapes are no longer double-counted by audit or feedback readers; persisted data is migrated, canonicalized, or cleaned so readers consume one authoritative outcome.
- [./memory-api/crates/session-api/src/store_tests.rs](./memory-api/crates/session-api/src/store_tests.rs) covers real migration/cleanup behavior and the overlapping-outcome regression.
- `rtk cargo test -p session-api --lib` passes after the follow-up implementation.
- Ticket/spec state reflects the new direction cleanly, without leaving approval-ready language attached to fallback-based behavior.
~~~
